quote: "You have power over your mind — not outside events. Realize this, and you will find strength."
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: "Waste no more time arguing what a good man should be. Be one."
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: "The best revenge is to be unlike him who performed the injury."
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: "If it is not right, do not do it; if it is not true, do not say it."
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: "When you arise in the morning, think of what a precious privilege it is to be alive — to breathe, to think, to enjoy, to love."
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: "Confine yourself to the present."
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: "The impediment to action advances action. What stands in the way becomes the way."
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: "Loss is nothing else but change, and change is Nature's delight."
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: "Begin each day by telling yourself: today I shall be meeting with interference, ingratitude, insolence, disloyalty, ill-will, and selfishness — all of them due to the offenders' ignorance of what is good or evil."
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: "Look well into thyself; there is a source of strength which will always spring up if thou wilt always look."
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: "Very little is needed to make a happy life; it is all within yourself, in your way of thinking."
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: "How much trouble he avoids who does not look to see what his neighbor says or does or thinks."
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: "Accept the things to which fate binds you, and love the people with whom fate brings you together, and do so with all your heart."
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: "Dwell on the beauty of life. Watch the stars, and see yourself running with them."
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: "Our life is what our thoughts make it."
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: "It is not what happens to you, but how you react to it that matters."
author: Epictetus

quote: "Wealth consists not in having great possessions, but in having few wants."
author: Epictetus

quote: "First say to yourself what you would be; and then do what you have to do."
author: Epictetus

quote: "Don't explain your philosophy. Embody it."
author: Epictetus

quote: "He who laughs at himself never runs out of things to laugh at."
author: Epictetus

quote: "Only the educated are free."
author: Epictetus

quote: "Make the best use of what is in your power, and take the rest as it happens."
author: Epictetus

quote: "We have two ears and one mouth so that we can listen twice as much as we speak."
author: Epictetus

quote: "Circumstances don't make the man; they only reveal him to himself."
author: Epictetus

quote: "If you want to improve, be content to be thought foolish and stupid."
author: Epictetus

quote: "It is not that we have a short time to live, but that we waste much of it."
author: Seneca

quote: "Sometimes even to live is an act of courage."
author: Seneca

quote: "Difficulties strengthen the mind, as labor does the body."
author: Seneca

quote: "Luck is what happens when preparation meets opportunity."
author: Seneca

quote: "If a man knows not which port he sails, no wind is favorable."
author: Seneca

quote: "We suffer more often in imagination than in reality."
author: Seneca

quote: "As long as you live, keep learning how to live."
author: Seneca

quote: "It is the power of the mind to be unconquerable."
author: Seneca

quote: "Wherever the art of medicine is loved, there is also a love of humanity."
author: Hippocrates

quote: "Life is short, the art long, opportunity fleeting, experience treacherous, judgment difficult."
author: Hippocrates

quote: "Healing is a matter of time, but it is sometimes also a matter of opportunity."
author: Hippocrates

quote: "Cure sometimes, treat often, comfort always."
author: Hippocrates

quote: "Make a habit of two things: to help; or at least to do no harm."
author: Hippocrates

quote: "Walking is a man's best medicine."
author: Hippocrates

quote: "Natural forces within us are the true healers of disease."
author: Hippocrates

quote: "The good physician treats the disease; the great physician treats the patient who has the disease."
author: William Osler

quote: "Listen to your patient, he is telling you the diagnosis."
author: William Osler

quote: "The trained nurse has become one of the greatest blessings of humanity."
author: William Osler

quote: "To study the phenomena of disease without books is to sail an uncharted sea; while to study books without patients is not to go to sea at all."
author: William Osler

quote: "The whole art of medicine is in observation."
author: William Osler

quote: "The greater the ignorance, the greater the dogmatism."
author: William Osler

quote: "Do the kind thing and do it first."
author: William Osler

quote: "Look wise, say nothing, and grunt. Speech was given to conceal thought."
author: William Osler

quote: "Whatever you are, be a good one."
author: Abraham Lincoln

quote: "I am a slow walker, but I never walk back."
author: Abraham Lincoln

quote: "The best thing about the future is that it comes one day at a time."
author: Abraham Lincoln

quote: "Nearly all men can stand adversity, but if you want to test a man's character, give him power."
author: Abraham Lincoln

quote: "Folks are usually about as happy as they make their minds up to be."
author: Abraham Lincoln

quote: "It is not the years in your life that count. It's the life in your years."
author: Abraham Lincoln

quote: "Most people are about as happy as they make up their minds to be."
author: Abraham Lincoln

quote: "Things may come to those who wait, but only the things left by those who hustle."
author: Abraham Lincoln

quote: "Be yourself; everyone else is already taken."
author: Oscar Wilde

quote: "Experience is the name everyone gives to their mistakes."
author: Oscar Wilde

quote: "I can resist anything except temptation."
author: Oscar Wilde

quote: "Some cause happiness wherever they go; others, whenever they go."
author: Oscar Wilde

quote: "The truth is rarely pure and never simple."
author: Oscar Wilde

quote: "Always forgive your enemies; nothing annoys them so much."
author: Oscar Wilde

quote: "Anyone who lives within their means suffers from a lack of imagination."
author: Oscar Wilde

quote: "Education is an admirable thing, but it is well to remember from time to time that nothing that is worth knowing can be taught."
author: Oscar Wilde

quote: "The two most important days in your life are the day you are born and the day you find out why."
author: Mark Twain

quote: "The secret of getting ahead is getting started."
author: Mark Twain

quote: "Twenty years from now you will be more disappointed by the things that you didn't do than by the ones you did do."
author: Mark Twain

quote: "Kindness is the language which the deaf can hear and the blind can see."
author: Mark Twain

quote: "If you tell the truth, you don't have to remember anything."
author: Mark Twain

quote: "The mass of men lead lives of quiet desperation."
author: Henry David Thoreau

quote: "Go confidently in the direction of your dreams. Live the life you have imagined."
author: Henry David Thoreau

quote: "Not all those who wander are lost."
author: J.R.R. Tolkien

quote: "In the midst of winter, I found there was, within me, an invincible summer."
author: Albert Camus

quote: "Anyone who has never made a mistake has never tried anything new."
author: Albert Einstein

quote: "Try not to become a person of success, but rather try to become a person of value."
author: Albert Einstein

quote: "Imagination is more important than knowledge."
author: Albert Einstein

quote: "That which does not kill us makes us stronger."
author: Friedrich Nietzsche

quote: "He who has a why to live for can bear almost any how."
author: Friedrich Nietzsche

quote: "The only way out is through."
author: Robert Frost

quote: "In three words I can sum up everything I've learned about life: it goes on."
author: Robert Frost

quote: "Life can only be understood backwards; but it must be lived forwards."
author: Søren Kierkegaard

quote: "Do not pray for an easy life. Pray for the strength to endure a difficult one."
author: Bruce Lee

quote: "If you want to lift yourself up, lift up someone else."
author: Booker T. Washington

quote: "It always seems impossible until it's done."
author: Nelson Mandela

quote: "Be kind, for everyone you meet is fighting a hard battle."
author: Ian Maclaren

quote: "Tell me, what is it you plan to do with your one wild and precious life?"
author: Mary Oliver

quote: "The world breaks everyone, and afterward, some are strong at the broken places."
author: Ernest Hemingway

quote: "Out of clutter, find simplicity. From discord, find harmony. In the middle of difficulty lies opportunity."
author: Albert Einstein

quote: "First, do no harm."
author: Hippocratic tradition

quote: "April is the cruelest month, breeding lilacs out of the dead land."
author: T.S. Eliot

quote: "Spring is the time of year when it is summer in the sun and winter in the shade."
author: Charles Dickens

quote: "Autumn is a second spring when every leaf is a flower."
author: Albert Camus

quote: "People don't notice whether it's winter or summer when they're happy."
author: Anton Chekhov

quote: "In the depth of winter, I finally learned that within me there lay an invincible summer."
author: Albert Camus

quote: "Adopt the pace of nature: her secret is patience."
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

quote: "The single biggest problem in communication is the illusion that it has taken place."
author: George Bernard Shaw

quote: "Alone we can do so little; together we can do so much."
author: Helen Keller

quote: "Coming together is a beginning. Keeping together is progress. Working together is success."
author: Henry Ford

quote: "If you want to go fast, go alone. If you want to go far, go together."
author: African proverb

quote: "Talent wins games, but teamwork and intelligence win championships."
author: Michael Jordan

quote: "In union there is strength."
author: Aesop

quote: "Gratitude turns what we have into enough."
author: Aesop

quote: "Mothers hold their children's hands for a short while, but their hearts forever."
author: Anonymous

quote: "A father is neither an anchor to hold us back nor a sail to take us there, but a guiding light whose love shows us the way."
author: Anonymous

quote: "Freedom is never more than one generation away from extinction."
author: Ronald Reagan
source: Address to the Phoenix Chamber of Commerce, 1961

quote: "Genius is one percent inspiration and ninety-nine percent perspiration."
author: Thomas Edison

quote: "It is hard to fail, but it is worse never to have tried to succeed."
author: Theodore Roosevelt

quote: "Do what you can, with what you have, where you are."
author: Theodore Roosevelt

quote: "Comparison is the thief of joy."
author: Theodore Roosevelt

quote: "Far and away the best prize that life offers is the chance to work hard at work worth doing."
author: Theodore Roosevelt

quote: "Habit, n. A shackle for the free."
author: Ambrose Bierce

quote: "Hurry, n. The dispatch of bunglers."
author: Ambrose Bierce

quote: "Advice, n. The smallest current coin."
author: Ambrose Bierce

quote: "Adage, n. Boned wisdom for weak teeth."
author: Ambrose Bierce

quote: "Accountability, n. The mother of caution."
author: Ambrose Bierce

quote: "Achievement, n. The death of endeavor and the birth of disgust."
author: Ambrose Bierce

quote: "Applause, n. The echo of a platitude."
author: Ambrose Bierce

quote: "Bore, n. A person who talks when you wish him to listen."
author: Ambrose Bierce

quote: "Patience, n. A minor form of despair, disguised as a virtue."
author: Ambrose Bierce

quote: "Politeness, n. The most acceptable hypocrisy."
author: Ambrose Bierce

quote: "Misfortune, n. The kind of fortune that never misses."
author: Ambrose Bierce

quote: "Hope, n. Desire and expectation rolled into one."
author: Ambrose Bierce

quote: "Predicament, n. The wage of consistency."
author: Ambrose Bierce

quote: "Lecturer, n. One with his hand in your pocket, his tongue in your ear, and his faith in your patience."
author: Ambrose Bierce

quote: "Twice, adv. Once too often."
author: Ambrose Bierce

quote: "Year, n. A period of three hundred and sixty-five disappointments."
author: Ambrose Bierce

quote: "Acquaintance, n. A person whom we know well enough to borrow from, but not well enough to lend to."
author: Ambrose Bierce

quote: "Egotist, n. A person of low taste — more interested in himself than in me."
author: Ambrose Bierce

quote: "Future, n. That period of time in which our affairs prosper, our friends are true, and our happiness is assured."
author: Ambrose Bierce

quote: "Distress, n. A disease incurred by exposure to the prosperity of a friend."
author: Ambrose Bierce

quote: "Telephone, n. An invention of the devil which abrogates some of the advantages of making a disagreeable person keep his distance."
author: Ambrose Bierce

quote: "Saint, n. A dead sinner revised and edited."
author: Ambrose Bierce

quote: "Education, n. That which discloses to the wise and disguises from the foolish their lack of understanding."
author: Ambrose Bierce

quote: "Conservative, n. A statesman who is enamored of existing evils, as distinguished from the Liberal, who wishes to replace them with others."
author: Ambrose Bierce

quote: "Diplomacy, n. The patriotic art of lying for one's country."
author: Ambrose Bierce

quote: "Mausoleum, n. The final and funniest folly of the rich."
author: Ambrose Bierce

quote: "Idleness, n. A model farm where the devil experiments with seeds of new sins."
author: Ambrose Bierce

quote: "Optimist, n. A proponent of the doctrine that black is white."
author: Ambrose Bierce

quote: "Pessimism, n. A philosophy forced upon the convictions of the observer by the disheartening prevalence of the optimist with his scarecrow hope and his unsightly smile."
author: Ambrose Bierce

quote: "Self-Evident, adj. Evident to one's self and to nobody else."
author: Ambrose Bierce

quote: "More, n. The comparative degree of too much."
author: Ambrose Bierce

quote: "Enough, n. All there is in the world if you like it."
author: Ambrose Bierce

quote: "Pleasure, n. The least hateful form of dejection."
author: Ambrose Bierce

quote: "Fashion, n. A despot whom the wise ridicule and obey."
author: Ambrose Bierce

quote: "Quotation, n. The act of repeating erroneously the words of another."
author: Ambrose Bierce

quote: "Resolute, adj. Obstinate in a course that we approve."
author: Ambrose Bierce

quote: "Cynic, n. A blackguard whose faulty vision sees things as they are, not as they ought to be."
author: Ambrose Bierce

quote: "Friendship, n. A ship big enough to carry two in fair weather, but only one in foul."
author: Ambrose Bierce

quote: "Patient, adj. Bearing the offences of others with composure, while plotting our own."
author: Ambrose Bierce

quote: "Whoever has lived long enough to find out what life is, knows how deep a debt of gratitude we owe to Adam, the first great benefactor of our race. He brought death into the world."
author: Mark Twain

quote: "Adam and Eve had many advantages, but the principal one was, that they escaped teething."
author: Mark Twain

quote: "Remark of Dr. Baldwin's, concerning upstarts: We don't care to eat toadstools that think they are truffles."
author: Mark Twain

quote: "Let us endeavor so to live that when we come to die even the undertaker will be sorry."
author: Mark Twain

quote: "Habit is habit, and not to be flung out of the window by any man, but coaxed down-stairs a step at a time."
author: Mark Twain

quote: "One of the most striking differences between a cat and a lie is that a cat has only nine lives."
author: Mark Twain

quote: "The holy passion of Friendship is of so sweet and steady and loyal and enduring a nature that it will last through a whole lifetime, if not asked to lend money."
author: Mark Twain

quote: "Consider well the proportions of things. It is better to be a young June-bug than an old bird of paradise."
author: Mark Twain

quote: "Why is it that we rejoice at a birth and grieve at a funeral? It is because we are not the person involved."
author: Mark Twain

quote: "If you pick up a starving dog and make him prosperous, he will not bite you. This is the principal difference between a dog and a man."
author: Mark Twain

quote: "Even popularity can be overdone. In Rome, along at first, you are full of regrets that Michelangelo died; but by and by you only regret that you didn't see him do it."
author: Mark Twain

quote: "Few things are harder to put up with than the annoyance of a good example."
author: Mark Twain

quote: "It were not best that we should all think alike; it is difference of opinion that makes horse-races."
author: Mark Twain

quote: "He is useless on top of the ground; he ought to be under it, inspiring the cabbages."
author: Mark Twain

quote: "April 1. This is the day upon which we are reminded of what we are on the other three hundred and sixty-four."
author: Mark Twain

quote: "It is often the case that the man who can't tell a lie thinks he is the best judge of one."
author: Mark Twain

quote: "The art of medicine consists of amusing the patient while nature cures the disease."
author: Voltaire

quote: "To do two things at once is to do neither."
author: Publilius Syrus

quote: "Nothing in life is to be feared, it is only to be understood."
author: Marie Curie

quote: "The beginning of wisdom is the definition of terms."
author: Socrates

quote: "We shall not cease from exploration, and the end of all our exploring will be to arrive where we started and know the place for the first time."
author: T.S. Eliot

quote: "Do not go where the path may lead; go instead where there is no path and leave a trail."
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

quote: "The cure for anything is salt water — sweat, tears, or the sea."
author: Isak Dinesen

quote: "In the middle of difficulty lies opportunity."
author: Albert Einstein

quote: "The greatest wealth is health."
author: Virgil

quote: "Nature itself is the best physician."
author: Hippocrates

quote: "I am not afraid of storms, for I am learning how to sail my ship."
author: Louisa May Alcott

quote: "The only way to do great work is to love what you do."
author: Steve Jobs

quote: "Attention is the rarest and purest form of generosity."
author: Simone Weil

quote: "It is not enough to be busy; so are the ants. The question is: what are we busy about?"
author: Henry David Thoreau

quote: "Out of your vulnerabilities will come your strength."
author: Sigmund Freud

quote: "Science is organized knowledge. Wisdom is organized life."
author: Immanuel Kant

quote: "The most important thing in communication is hearing what isn't said."
author: Peter Drucker

quote: "To know what you know and what you do not know, that is true knowledge."
author: Confucius

quote: "Excellence is never an accident. It is always the result of high intention, sincere effort, and intelligent execution."
author: Aristotle

quote: "The best preparation for tomorrow is doing your best today."
author: H. Jackson Brown Jr.

quote: "Time is the coin of your life. Be careful lest you let other people spend it for you."
author: Carl Sandburg

quote: "The function of the human body is to carry the brain around."
author: Thomas Edison

quote: "In character, in manner, in style, in all things, the supreme excellence is simplicity."
author: Henry Wadsworth Longfellow

quote: "The quality of a person's life is in direct proportion to their commitment to excellence."
author: Vince Lombardi

quote: "He who has health has hope, and he who has hope has everything."
author: Arabian proverb

quote: "The whole secret of a successful life is to find out what is one's destiny to do, and then do it."
author: Henry Ford

quote: "Not everything that can be counted counts, and not everything that counts can be counted."
author: William Bruce Cameron

quote: "Medicine is not only a science; it is also an art."
author: Paracelsus

quote: "The physician's highest calling is to make sick people healthy."
author: Samuel Hahnemann

quote: "A good laugh and a long sleep are the best cures in the doctor's book."
author: Irish proverb

quote: "The human body is the best picture of the human soul."
author: Ludwig Wittgenstein

quote: "Knowing is not enough; we must apply. Willing is not enough; we must do."
author: Johann Wolfgang von Goethe

quote: "The secret of change is to focus all of your energy not on fighting the old, but on building the new."
author: Socrates

quote: "I would rather have questions that cannot be answered than answers that cannot be questioned."
author: Richard Feynman

quote: "Every patient carries her or his own doctor inside."
author: Albert Schweitzer

quote: "The measure of intelligence is the ability to change."
author: Albert Einstein

quote: "Health is not valued till sickness comes."
author: Thomas Fuller

quote: "Wherever you go, go with all your heart."
author: Confucius

quote: "The greatest mistake in the treatment of diseases is that there are physicians for the body and physicians for the soul, although the two cannot be separated."
author: Plato

quote: "Without continual growth and progress, such words as improvement, achievement, and success have no meaning."
author: Benjamin Franklin

quote: "We know what we are, but know not what we may be."
author: William Shakespeare

quote: "It is health that is real wealth, and not pieces of gold and silver."
author: Mahatma Gandhi

quote: "The art is long, life is short, opportunity fleeting, experience treacherous, judgment difficult."
author: Hippocrates

quote: "What we learn with pleasure we never forget."
author: Alfred Mercier

quote: "The greatest medicine of all is teaching people how not to need it."
author: Hippocrates

quote: "Real generosity toward the future lies in giving all to the present."
author: Albert Camus

quote: "The secret of success is constancy of purpose."
author: Benjamin Disraeli

quote: "A word of kindness is seldom spoken in vain, while witty sayings are as easily lost as the pearls slipping from a broken string."
author: George Prentice
